title: Translation Units, Declarations, Diagnostics, and Linking · C Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A safety board presents unfamiliar translation units, declarations, diagnostics, and linking decisions across code, diagrams, diagnostics, and stakeholder evidence; each distractor corresponds to a named misconception. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for warning-clean multi-file interface dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Predict macro expansion, conditional compilation, include guards, and header ownership before translation.
Distinguish declarations, tentative and full definitions, linkage, compatible types, and one-definition responsibilities.
Use complete prototypes and compatible parameter and return types across every caller boundary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Preprocessing Boundaries · changed case · Quiz Preprocessing Boundaries
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz native-systems team handles memory case 3905. Produce bounded C evidence to model translation and diagnostic gates before execution; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Predict macro expansion, conditional compilation, include guards, and header ownership before translation.
